cartMetaDescription: Anonymous storefront cart backed by the live Darwin.WebApi public cart contracts.
checkoutMetaDescription: Public storefront checkout backed by live intent, order, and payment contracts.
confirmationMetaDescription: Storefront order confirmation and hosted checkout reconciliation view.
cartHeroTitle: Storefront cart is now a real public commerce slice
cartHeroDescription: This cart runs against the public Darwin.WebApi cart endpoints and keeps anonymous storefront identity stable through a dedicated web-owned cookie.
cartItemAdded: The item was added to the cart.
cartItemRemoved: The item was removed from the cart.
cartAddFailedMessage: The item could not be added to the cart.
cartUpdateInvalidMessage: The cart update request is invalid.
cartUpdateFailedMessage: The cart quantity could not be updated.
cartRemoveInvalidMessage: The cart remove request is invalid.
cartRemoveFailedMessage: The item could not be removed from the cart.
cartCouponMissingCartIdMessage: The coupon request is missing the cart identifier.
cartCouponApplyFailedMessage: The coupon could not be applied.
storefrontCartNotFoundMessage: The storefront cart resource was not found.
storefrontCartHttpErrorMessage: The storefront cart request could not be completed.
storefrontCartNetworkErrorMessage: The storefront cart API could not be reached.
cartDegradedTitle: Cart is running in degraded mode.
cartRouteSummaryMessage: Cart status: {status}. {itemCount} cart lines are currently visible. Follow-up product count: {followUpCount}.
cartOpportunityMessage: {count} follow-up products are currently visible, so the cart can still surface the strongest adjacent buying opportunity before checkout.
cartOpportunityFallback: No adjacent offer signal is currently visible, so the cart stays focused on protecting the active basket before checkout.
cartOpportunityOfferMessage: This is the strongest visible follow-up offer right now at {savingsPercent}% savings.
emptyCartTitle: No items are in the storefront cart yet.
emptyCartDescription: Add a product from a detail page to create an anonymous storefront cart and validate the public cart contracts end to end.
couponPlaceholder: Enter coupon or leave blank to clear
cartMemberCheckoutMessage: Member address status: {status}. {count} reusable addresses are currently available for checkout prefill.
cartMemberCheckoutEmptyMessage: No reusable member addresses are available yet. You can still continue with manual checkout entry or add addresses in the member portal.
cartMemberContextMessage: Profile status: {profileStatus}. Preferences status: {preferencesStatus}. Address-book status: {addressesStatus}. Cart should keep the authenticated member context visible before checkout starts.
checkoutSummaryNote: Public checkout now sits on top of this cart using the live intent, order-placement, and confirmation contracts from Darwin.WebApi.
followUpProductsDescription: The cart now keeps a small storefront follow-up rail visible so the shopper can keep browsing without losing the current cart context.
followUpProductFallbackDescription: Published storefront product available for quick follow-up from the cart.
followUpProductsUnavailableMessage: No additional published storefront products are available for follow-up right now.
cartNextStepsDescription: The storefront cart stays explicit about what the shopper should validate before leaving this page.
cartNextStepReviewMessage: Check quantities, applied coupon state, and line totals here before moving the shopper into authoritative checkout preview.
cartNextStepCheckoutTitle: Move to shipping and order preview
cartNextStepCheckoutMessage: Checkout uses the live intent contract to calculate shipping options and totals after address capture.
cartCrossSurfaceMessage: The cart should stay connected to the wider storefront system so the shopper can move between browsing, checkout, and account follow-up without losing context.
commerceContinuationMessage: Commerce routes should keep clear handoff paths into browsing, account follow-up, content context, and conversion recovery instead of ending in route-local CTA clusters.
commerceContinuationHomeDescription: Use the public home route to reorient around CMS, catalog, and member entry flows.
commerceContinuationCatalogDescription: Move back into published product discovery without losing the broader storefront context.
commerceContinuationCmsDescription: Use published content pages when the shopper needs broader storefront context beyond commerce routes.
commerceContinuationCartDescription: Go back to the current anonymous storefront cart to review quantities, coupon state, and line totals.
commerceContinuationAccountDescription: Keep self-service and member-portal follow-up visible alongside the storefront commerce flow.
commerceAuthCartTitle: Save cart progress in account follow-up
commerceAuthCartDescription: Public cart can continue without sign-in, but account self-service should stay one step away when the shopper wants to preserve the active conversion path.
commerceAuthCheckoutTitle: Open account help for checkout continuation
commerceAuthCheckoutDescription: Checkout can stay public, but account entry should remain visible for sign-in, registration, activation, and password recovery without dropping the current conversion intent.
commerceAuthProductOfferDescription: This is the strongest visible offer right now at {savingsPercent}% savings, currently available for {price}.
commerceAuthProductFallbackDescription: A live product opportunity is available directly from the current public catalog contract.
commerceAuthProductEmptyMessage: No live product highlights are currently visible from this commerce handoff. Current status: {status}.
commerceAuthOfferBoardMessage: Visible product opportunities: {productCount}. Product status: {status}. Guest commerce handoff should keep multiple buying options visible while the shopper decides whether to sign in or create an account.
commerceAuthOfferBoardOfferDescription: {campaignLabel} currently visible at {savingsPercent}% savings, available for {price}.
commerceAuthOfferBoardFallbackDescriptio: A live buying opportunity is available directly from the current public catalog contract.
commerceAuthOfferBoardEmptyMessage: No live product opportunities are currently visible from this commerce handoff. Current status: {status}.
checkoutUnavailableTitle: Checkout is unavailable without a cart.
checkoutUnavailableMessage: Add at least one published storefront product before starting the public checkout flow.
checkoutHeroTitle: Checkout now runs on the live storefront intent and order contracts
checkoutHeroDescription: Enter the delivery address, review the rated shipping options, and place an order through the public Darwin.WebApi checkout flow.
checkoutAddressIncompleteErrorMessage: Shipping and billing address fields are incomplete.
checkoutPlaceOrderFailedMessage: The order could not be placed.
checkoutHostedCheckoutStartFailedMessage: Hosted checkout could not be started.
checkoutPaymentCompletionFailedMessage: Hosted checkout return could not be reconciled.
storefrontCheckoutNotFoundMessage: The storefront checkout resource was not found.
storefrontCheckoutHttpErrorMessage: The storefront checkout request could not be completed.
storefrontCheckoutNetworkErrorMessage: The storefront checkout API could not be reached.
addressIncompleteMessage: Fill the required fields and refresh the checkout preview to calculate authoritative shipping and totals.
checkoutRouteSummaryMessage: Cart status: {cartStatus}. Intent status: {intentStatus}. {lineCount} cart lines are currently in scope. Address readiness: {addressReady}.
checkoutConfidenceReadyMessage: The main checkout prerequisites are in place, so the shopper can move toward authoritative order placement with fewer unknowns.
checkoutConfidencePendingMessage: Checkout is still active, but one or more prerequisites need attention before the route should be treated as order-ready.
checkoutPaymentWindowMessage: Before order placement, checkout should keep the payment handoff, projected payable total, and any open billing attention visible instead of leaving them implicit.
checkoutPaymentWindowRoutePending: Waiting for a complete checkout preview
checkoutPaymentWindowAccountGuest: Guest checkout remains active with account help available
checkoutPaymentWindowBillingEmpty: No open billing attention is visible
savedAddressDescription: When a member session is available, checkout can reuse the canonical address book instead of forcing the shopper to retype every address field.
savedAddressEmptyMessage: No reusable member addresses are currently available. You can still continue with manual checkout entry or add addresses in the member portal.
savedProfilePrefillMessage: Member profile status: {status}. Checkout can still reuse canonical profile identity while the address book is empty.
savedProfilePrefillActiveMessage: The current checkout draft was prefilled from the member profile because no saved address was selected.
memberCheckoutContextTitle: Keep profile, channels, and address readiness visible in checkout
memberCheckoutContextDescription: Profile status: {profileStatus}. Preferences status: {preferencesStatus}. Address-book status: {addressesStatus}. Checkout should stay connected to the authenticated member context instead of treating member prefill as hidden background state.
memberCheckoutSelectedAddressReady: A saved address is currently selected for checkout.
memberCheckoutNoSelectedAddress: No saved address is currently selected for this checkout draft.
memberCheckoutFinanceTitle: Keep invoice attention visible before placing a new order
memberCheckoutFinanceDescription: Invoices status: {invoicesStatus}. {count} recent invoices are currently available to the signed-in shopper, so checkout can stay aware of open billing follow-up instead of acting in isolation.
memberCheckoutFinanceInvoiceEmpty: No invoice follow-up is currently visible
deliveryAddressDescription: This first slice keeps billing and shipping aligned to one address so the storefront checkout can stay small and contract-first.
shippingOptionsDescription: Select one option and refresh the preview before placing the order.
checkoutReadinessDescription: These signals reflect whether the current storefront draft is ready for authoritative preview and order placement.
shippingSelectionMissingMessage: Choose a shipping option in the preview form and refresh checkout before placing the order.
noPreviewMessage: The order button becomes available after the address is complete and the public checkout intent succeeds.
shippingNotRequiredMessage: The current checkout intent does not require a shipping selection for this order.
checkoutLinesDescription: The checkout summary now keeps the live cart lines visible so the shopper can verify products, quantities, and line totals before order placement.
placeOrderDescription: This creates the order through the public checkout endpoint and then moves the shopper into the confirmation and payment handoff flow.
checkoutCrossSurfaceMessage: Checkout is authoritative, but it should still stay connected to the rest of the storefront journey instead of becoming an isolated form route.
orderConfirmationUnavailableMessage: The public confirmation endpoint returned status "{status}".
orderConfirmationTitle: Order {orderNumber} is now on the storefront timeline
orderConfirmationDescription: This screen reads the authoritative storefront confirmation contract from Darwin.WebApi and keeps payment handoff visible instead of hiding it behind placeholder text.
orderPlacedMessage: The cart was successfully converted into an order. The next step is payment handoff unless the order is already marked as paid.
missingContextTitle: Payment return could not be finalized automatically.
missingContextMessage: The web-owned payment handoff context was missing, so the confirmation page can only show the latest order snapshot.
hostedCheckoutCancelledMessage: The shopper returned from the payment handoff without completing payment. The order remains visible here and payment can be retried.
confirmationRouteSummaryMessage: Confirmation status: {status}. Payment completion status: {paymentCompletionStatus}. Latest payment status: {paymentStatus}.
confirmationPaymentWindowMessage: Confirmation should keep the visible payment footprint clear so payment recovery, provider visibility, and total commercial exposure stay understandable after checkout.
confirmationPaymentWindowRouteDone: No further payment handoff is needed
confirmationPaymentWindowCoverageComplet: The visible payment coverage is complete
confirmationPaymentWindowCoverageOpen: The visible payment coverage is still open
confirmationCareAttentionMessage: This order still needs payment or account follow-up attention, so the confirmation route keeps the operational basics visible instead of acting like a passive receipt.
confirmationCareStableMessage: This order is in a stable post-purchase state, so the confirmation route can shift focus from payment recovery to follow-up and repeat engagement.
confirmationNextWindowMemberMessage: Signed-in shoppers can continue directly into orders, invoices, and loyalty, so the confirmation route can act as a real member handoff instead of a dead end.
confirmationNextWindowGuestMessage: Guest shoppers can still move into account-based follow-up after this order, so the confirmation route keeps the next customer window visible even before sign-in.
confirmationNextWindowOrdersFallback: Order history follow-up starts after account access
confirmationNextWindowBillingFallback: No open billing attention is currently visible
confirmationNextWindowLoyaltyFallback: No immediate loyalty follow-up is currently visible
nextStepsDescription: The confirmation route now keeps the immediate follow-up actions visible so payment, account access, and order reference handling are explicit after checkout.
nextStepPaymentRetryMessage: Retry the hosted checkout handoff from this confirmation page to finish payment for the order.
nextStepPaymentPendingMessage: The order exists already, but payment is not recorded yet. Continue to the provider checkout to complete the storefront flow.
nextStepPaymentDoneMessage: No further payment step is required. The confirmation snapshot can now act as the stable handoff into order follow-up.
nextStepAccountSignedInTitle: Order follow-up is available in the member portal
nextStepAccountSignedInMessage: Because this browser already has a member session, you can move straight into the orders area for future tracking and payment follow-up.
nextStepAccountGuestTitle: Sign in if you want account-based tracking
nextStepAccountGuestMessage: The confirmation page remains public, but order history and invoice follow-up live in the member portal after sign-in or account creation.
nextStepReferenceMessage: Use the order number for support, payment retry, or future member-portal lookup so the storefront and support timelines stay aligned.
confirmationMemberWindowMessage: Orders status: {ordersStatus}. Invoices status: {invoicesStatus}. Loyalty status: {loyaltyStatus}. Signed-in shoppers should be able to continue from confirmation straight into the member portal without losing the post-checkout timeline.
confirmationMemberOrdersEmpty: No recent member orders are currently visible
confirmationMemberInvoicesEmpty: No invoice follow-up is currently visible
confirmationMemberLoyaltyEmpty: No loyalty follow-up is currently visible
confirmationGuestWindowMessage: The order confirmation remains public, but account-based tracking continues after sign-in or account creation. The protected return route is prepared as {returnPath}.
confirmationGuestProductOfferDescription: This is the strongest visible post-purchase offer right now at {savingsPercent}% savings, currently available for {price}.
confirmationGuestProductFallbackDescript: A live product opportunity is ready once the shopper wants to continue beyond account tracking.
confirmationGuestProductEmptyMessage: No live product highlight is currently visible from guest confirmation.
confirmationGuestOfferBoardMessage: Visible post-purchase opportunities: {productCount}. Guest confirmation should keep several next-buy options visible while the shopper decides whether to continue into account-based tracking.
confirmationGuestOfferBoardOfferDescript: {campaignLabel} currently visible at {savingsPercent}% savings, available for {price}.
confirmationGuestOfferBoardFallbackDescr: A live post-purchase buying opportunity is ready once the shopper wants to continue beyond account tracking.
confirmationGuestOfferBoardEmptyMessage: No live post-purchase opportunities are currently visible from guest confirmation.
noPaymentAttemptsMessage: No payment attempts are attached yet.
paymentRecordedMessage: No additional payment handoff is required for this order.
confirmationCrossSurfaceMessage: The confirmation route should hand the shopper cleanly into browsing, account follow-up, or broader storefront orientation instead of ending as a receipt-only dead end.
storefrontWindowMessage: CMS status: {cmsPagesStatus}. Category status: {categoriesStatus}. Product status: {productsStatus}. {cmsCount} published pages, {categoryCount} public categories, and {productCount} product opportunities are currently visible, so the shopper can keep content and buying discovery close to the active purchase flow.
storefrontWindowCmsFallbackMessage: Use public CMS content to keep broader storefront context visible while the shopper stays close to cart and checkout.
storefrontWindowCatalogFallbackMessage: Use category-led browse paths when the shopper is ready to expand the basket without losing the live purchase timeline.
storefrontWindowProductOfferBoardMessage: Visible product opportunities: {productCount}. Product status: {status}. Active commerce routes should keep several strongest buying options visible while the shopper stays close to cart, checkout, and post-purchase follow-up.
storefrontWindowProductOfferBoardDescrip: {campaignLabel} currently visible at {savingsPercent}% savings, available for {price}.
storefrontWindowProductOfferBoardFallbac: A live buying opportunity is available directly from the current public catalog contract.
storefrontWindowProductOfferBoardEmptyMe: No live product opportunities are currently visible from this commerce discovery window. Current status: {status}.
storefrontWindowCampaignMessage: {categoryCount} visible categories and {productCount} visible products are enough to build stronger browse and buying lanes from the active commerce route.
storefrontWindowCampaignCategoryFallback: This visible category is ready to carry the shopper into a stronger browse lane.
storefrontWindowCampaignProductDescripti: This visible product carries a savings signal of {savingsPercent}% at {price}.
storefrontWindowCampaignProductFallbackD: This visible product is already live at {price} and ready for the next buying move.
storefrontWindowCampaignEmptyMessage: No stronger browse campaigns are currently derivable from this commerce route.
confirmationStorefrontWindowMessage: Confirmation now also keeps live content, browse, and product opportunities visible so the shopper can continue into the next commercial step after the order lands.
